author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
verses:
tapanamiśrera putra raghunātha bhaṭṭācārya
prabhuke dekhite cale chāḍi’ sarvakārya

kāśī hoite calilā gauḍapatha diyā
saṅgete sevaka eka cale jhā̐pi laiyā

eimate raghunātha āilā nīlācale
mahāprabhura caraṇe giyā mililā kutūhale

daṇḍavat praṇāma kari’ paḍilā caraṇe
prabhu raghunātha jāni’ kailā āliṅgane

‘‘bhāla haila āilā, dekha kamalalocana
āji āmāra ihā karibā prasāda-bhojana”

govindere kahi eka vāsā deoyāilā
svarūpādi bhaktagaṇa⋅sane milāilā

eimate prabhu⋅sane rahilā aṣṭamāsa
dine dine prabhu kṛpāya bāḍaye ullāsa

madhye madhye mahāprabhura karena nimantraṇa
ghara⋅bhāta karena, āra vividha vyañjana

raghunātha bhaṭṭa⋅pāke ati sunipuṇa
jei rāndhe, sei haya amṛtera sama

parama santoṣe prabhu karena bhojana
prabhura avaśiṣṭa pātra bhaṭṭera bhakṣaṇa

aṣṭamāsa rahi’ prabhu bhaṭṭe vidāya dilā
‘vivāha nā kariha’ bali’ niṣedha karilā

“vṛddha pitāmātāra jāi’ karaha sevana
vaiṣṇava⋅pāśa bhāgavata kara adhyayana

punarapi ekabāra āsiha nīlācale”
eta kahi’ kaṇṭhamālā dila tā’ra gale

āliṅgana kari’, prabhu tā’re vidāya dilā
preme gadagada bhaṭṭa kā̐dite lāgilā

svarūpa⋅ādi bhakta ṭhā̐i ājñā māgiyā
vārāṇasī āilā bhaṭṭa prabhu⋅ājñā pāiyā

cāri vatsara ghare pitāmātāra sevā kailā
vaiṣṇava⋅paṇḍita ṭhā̐i bhāgavata paḍilā

pitāmātā kāśī pāile udāsīna haiyā
punaḥ prabhura ṭhā̐i gela bhāvete galiyā

pūrvavat aṣṭamāsa prabhupāśa chilā
aṣṭamāsa rahi’ prabhu punaḥ ājñā dilā

“āmāra ājñāya raghunātha, jāha vṛndāvane
tathā giyā raha rūpa⋅sanātana⋅sane

bhāgavata paḍa sadā laha kṛṣṇa⋅nāma
acire karibena kṛpā kṛṣṇa bhagavān”

eta bali’ prabhu tā̐’re āliṅgana kailā
prabhura kṛpāte kṛṣṇapreme matta hailā

cauddahāta jagannāthera tulasīra mālā
chuṭā⋅pāna⋅ciḍā⋅mahotsave pāñāchilā

sei mālā, chuṭā⋅pāna prabhu tā̐’re dilā
iṣṭadeva kari’ mālā dhariyā rākhilā

prabhu ṭhā̐i ājñā pāñā āilā vṛndāvane
āśraya karilā āsi’ rūpa⋅sanātane

rūpagosāira sabhāya kare bhāgavata⋅paṭhana
bhāgavata paḍite preme āulāya mana

aśru⋅kampa⋅gadagada prabhura kṛpāte
netre aśru, ruddha⋅kaṇṭha nā pāre paḍite

pikasvara kaṇṭha tā’te rāgera vibhāga
eka śloka paḍite phirāya cāri rāga

kṛṣṇera mādhuryaguṇa jabe paḍe mane
premete vihvala haiyā kichui nā jāne

govindera caraṇe kaila ātmasamarpaṇa
govinda⋅caraṇāravinda jā̐’ra prāṇadhana

nija śiṣye kahi govinda⋅mandira korāilā
vaṁśī, makara, kuṇḍalādi bhūṣaṇa kari dilā

grāmavārtā nā śune, nā kahe jihvāya
kṛṣṇakathā⋅pūjādite aṣṭaprahara jāya

vaiṣṇavera nindyakarma nāhi pāḍe kāṇe
sabe kṛṣṇa bhajana kare⋅eimātra jāne

mahāprabhura datta mālā mananera kāle
prasāda⋅kaḍāra soi bāndhi lana gale

mahāprabhura kṛpāya kṛṣṇaprema anargala
mahāprabhura raghunāthe kṛpā⋅mahāphala
en_translation:
Tapanamiśra's son, Śrīla Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī, set out to see the Lord, leaving all other duties behind.

From Kāśī he journeyed by the path through Bengal, accompanied by a servant.

In this way, Raghunātha arrived at Jagannātha Purī and eagerly met Mahāprabhu at His feet.

Performing full obeisances, he fell at the Lord's feet, and the Lord, knowing Raghunātha, embraced him.

"It is good that you have come; behold the lotus-eyed Lord Jagannātha. Today you will partake of sanctified food with Me."

The Lord told Govinda to arrange a dwelling for him and introduced him to Svarūpa Dāmodara and other devotees.

In this manner, he remained with the Lord for eight months, and day by day his joy increased by the Lord's mercy.

From time to time, he would invite Mahāprabhu to his home and cook various delicious preparations.

Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa was very skilled in cooking; whatever he prepared was like nectar.

With supreme satisfaction the Lord would eat, and Raghunātha would then partake of the Lord's remnants.

After eight months, the Lord bade him farewell and instructed him: "Do not marry.

Go and serve your elderly parents, and study the Śrīmad Bhāgavatam with a devotee scholar.

Come again to Purī once more." Saying this, the Lord placed a garland around his neck.

Embracing him, the Lord bade him farewell, and Raghunātha, overwhelmed with love, began to weep.

Taking leave from Svarūpa Dāmodara and the other devotees, he returned to Varanasi.

For four years he served his parents at home and studied the Bhāgavatam with a devotee scholar.

When his parents passed away, he became detached and again went to the Lord, melting in divine emotion.

As before, he stayed with the Lord for eight months, after which the Lord again gave him an order:

"By My command, Raghunātha, go to Vṛndāvana and reside there with Rūpa and Sanātana.

Always read the Bhāgavatam and chant the Holy Names of Lord Kṛṣṇa; soon the Lord will bestow His mercy upon you."

Saying this, the Lord embraced him, and by the Lord's mercy, he became intoxicated with divine love.

He had received a fourteen-cubit Tulasī garland from Lord Jagannātha, along with leftover betel leaves and rice from a festival.

The Lord gave him those items, and he kept the garland, considering it his worshipable Deity.

Receiving the Lord's command, he came to Vṛndāvana and took shelter of Rūpa and Sanātana Gosvāmīs.

In Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī's assembly he would read the Bhāgavatam, and while reading, his mind would become overwhelmed with love.

With tears, trembling, and a choked voice, he could sometimes not even continue reading.

His voice was sweet like a cuckoo's, and he was expert in the melodic modes; while reading one verse, he would express four different melodies.

When Lord Kṛṣṇa's sweet qualities came to his mind, he would be so overwhelmed with love that he knew nothing else.

He surrendered at the feet of Lord Govinda, who was his life's treasure.

He instructed his disciples to build a temple for Lord Govinda and had ornaments made for the Deity.

He would not listen to worldly gossip, nor would he speak it; his twenty-four hours were spent in worship and topics about Lord Kṛṣṇa.

He would not hear any criticism of the devotees; he only knew that everyone was engaged in the worship of the Lord.

During meditation, he would wear the garland given by Mahāprabhu and keep a token of the Lord's remnants around his neck.

By Mahāprabhu's mercy, divine love flowed from him unimpeded. The Lord's mercy upon Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa was indeed a great fruit.
